body.title: [sprint] Finish the .bashrc -> uu_bash_profile.sh migration; strip UUMOVED debris + AI exports
body.intention: I intend that ~/.bashrc contains no AI/Igor/Anthropic/OpenRouter/Ollama/UnseenUniversity exports or aliases — everything AI-related lives in ~/.unseen_university/uu_bash_profile.sh (or its processor) — so .bashrc is a clean personal shell config and the UU env has one home.
body.description:
The .bashrc->uu_bash_profile.sh migration (D-uu-config-identity-layer-2026-06-22) was started but left incomplete. Debris/leftovers in ~/.bashrc: the `#UUMOVED#` commented exports (lines ~150,151,161,164 — CC_TMUX_SESSION, IGOR_INSTANCE_ID, UU_HOME_DB_URL, CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS), the still-live `export CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT=1` (line 154), the `cd ~/dev/src/UnseenUniversity` (line 143), the Igor-DB comment block (159-160), and the uurecall/uuresearch/compact-igor aliases+functions (167,185,188). Move the still-wanted AI/UU items into uu_bash_profile.sh; DELETE the dead #UUMOVED# comment lines. .bashrc should end with only the `source uu_bash_profile.sh` line (149) + Akien's non-UU personal config.

Note: the launcher now clears CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT itself (T-launcher-1m-default-fresh-session), so wherever the hygiene =1 lives, the launcher wins; keep the =1 default in uu_bash_profile.sh for non-launcher shells.

**Affected files:** ~/.bashrc (personal — back up first), ~/.unseen_university/uu_bash_profile.sh.
**Design rules:** none apply (shell config; secrets handled by T-consolidate-local-secrets-one-home).
**Scope boundary:** IN — move non-secret AI/UU exports+aliases to uu_bash_profile.sh, delete #UUMOVED# debris from .bashrc. OUT — the two exposed SECRETS (that is T-consolidate-local-secrets-one-home); deleting the .bak/.env debris files (T-remove-env-creds-debris).
**Test plan:** no automated tests (personal dotfile). Acceptance: `grep -niE 'anthropic|openrouter|ollama|igor|unseen|CC_TMUX|CC_WORKFLOW|UU_|CLAUDE_CODE' ~/.bashrc` returns only the single source-line; a fresh interactive shell still resolves UU_ROOT/CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS via the processor.
**Completion criteria:** .bashrc has no UU/AI exports/aliases beyond the source line; a fresh shell still has the full UU env via uu_bash_profile.sh + processor.
**Rollback:** .bashrc backup restores prior state (manual — outside git).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
